title: MekikConnectorOptions
description: Constructor options for MekikConnector — client adapter for mekik servers (Mekik Wire Protocol v1). Source: packages/connector-mekik/src/index.ts.

url.description: mekik WebSocket endpoint URL (ws:// or wss://).

protocols.description: Sub-protocol(s) passed to the WebSocket constructor.

reconnect.description: Auto-reconnect when the socket closes. Suppressed after an auth rejection (close code 4401).

reconnectDelay.description: Milliseconds to wait between reconnect attempts.

maxReconnectAttempts.description: Stop reconnecting after this many consecutive failures. Reset on a successful handshake.

queueOfflineMessages.description: Queue outgoing messages while the socket is down and flush them on (re)connect.

userId.description: Stable user identity sent in the hello handshake. Omit to let the server mint one (announced via the welcome frame). An authenticating server may override this with the verified identity.

conversationId.description: Conversation to resume. Omit to start a new one.

resumeConversation.description: Persist identity + watermark in localStorage and resume across page reloads.

auth.description: MekikAuthProvider adapter deciding what credential this client presents (CookieAuth, TokenAuth, or a custom provider). Cannot be serialised — set programmatically. Takes precedence over `token`.

token.description: Deprecated — shorthand for `auth: new TokenAuth({ token, maxRetries: 0 })`: a credential sent in the hello handshake with no retry on rejection. A function returning a fresh token per attempt is also accepted, but cannot be serialised — set that programmatically. Not needed for cookie-based auth.

onAuthError.description: Callback invoked when the server rejects the connection (error frame + close code 4401). Cannot be serialised — set programmatically.
